title: Coalition Building
shortSummary: Build and maintain political coalitions — negotiate with allied and rival factions, manage crossbench relationships, and assemble the numbers to pass legislation or win government.
whyItMatters: No politician governs alone. In Australia's multi-party system, the ability to build coalitions — within your party, across the chamber, and in the community — determines whether you can actually deliver on your promises.
coreConcepts: Coalition theory and minimum winning coalitions
The maths of majority: counting numbers in chambers
